retry_reason: Allocation failure (NULL return) is due to memory pressure, which may be transient; caller can retry later.
contract_summary: Allocates a btrfs_chunk_map and sets its initial refcount to 1, establishing ownership for the caller. The rb_node is cleared. The caller owns the returned reference and must eventually release it via a corresponding put (e.g., btrfs_free_chunk_map). If allocation fails (returns NULL), the caller should handle the error or retry. This wrapper represents the creation stage of the chunk_map lifecycle.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is allocation and initialization of refcount to 1 (set wrapper). The only logic is allocation, refcount_set, and RB_CLEAR_NODE — minimal and focused on lifecycle management.

check.reason: refcount_set(&map->refs, 1) sets the reference count to 1 on the newly allocated map. map is the return value, and the access path map.refs.refs correctly traces from the returned pointer through the refs field of btrfs_chunk_map (of type refcount_t) to the refs field of refcount_struct (atomic_t). The type chain is consistent with the callee info and code. The segment counts match: 3 segments (map, refs, refs) and 3 types (btrfs_chunk_map, refcount_struct, atomic_t).
